title: Basketball
description: 15 questions about NBA history, legendary players, records, international basketball, and Lithuanian basketball

questions.question: Who holds the NBA record for most points scored in a single game?
questions.explanation: Wilt Chamberlain scored 100 points for the Philadelphia Warriors against the New York Knicks on March 2, 1962.

questions.question: Which team has won the most NBA championships?
questions.explanation: The Boston Celtics have won 18 NBA championships, the most in league history.

questions.question: In which year did Lithuania's national basketball team win their first Olympic medal after independence?
questions.explanation: Lithuania won the bronze medal at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics, their first Games as an independent nation, famously wearing Grateful Dead tie-dye jerseys.

questions.question: Who is the NBA's all-time leading scorer?
questions.explanation: LeBron James surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's record in February 2023, becoming the NBA's all-time leading scorer.

questions.question: Which Lithuanian player was the first to play in the NBA?
questions.explanation: Sarunas Marciulionis joined the Golden State Warriors in 1989, becoming the first Lithuanian (and one of the first Soviets) to play in the NBA.

questions.question: What is the name of the famous 1992 US Olympic basketball team?
questions.explanation: The 1992 US Olympic team, featuring Michael Jordan, Magic Johnson, and Larry Bird, was nicknamed 'The Dream Team.'

questions.question: Which player won 6 NBA Finals MVP awards, all with the Chicago Bulls?
questions.explanation: Michael Jordan won 6 NBA Finals MVP awards across the Bulls' two three-peats (1991-93 and 1996-98).

questions.question: How many Olympic bronze medals has Lithuania's men's basketball team won?
questions.explanation: Lithuania won Olympic bronze medals in 1992 (Barcelona), 1996 (Atlanta), and 2000 (Sydney).

questions.question: Which NBA team went 73-9 in the 2015-16 regular season, setting the all-time wins record?
questions.explanation: The Golden State Warriors went 73-9 in 2015-16, surpassing the 1995-96 Bulls' 72-10 record, but lost the Finals to Cleveland.

questions.question: Which country won the first ever FIBA Basketball World Cup (then called the World Championship) in 1950?
questions.explanation: Argentina won the first FIBA World Championship in 1950, held in Buenos Aires.

questions.question: Arvydas Sabonis is often considered Lithuania's greatest basketball player. At what age did he finally join the NBA?
questions.explanation: Sabonis joined the Portland Trail Blazers in 1995 at age 31, well past his physical prime due to injuries and the Iron Curtain.

questions.question: Which player holds the record for the most assists in NBA history?
questions.explanation: John Stockton recorded 15,806 assists in his career, a record that is considered nearly unbreakable.

questions.question: In the controversial 1972 Olympic basketball final, which team defeated the USA in the last 3 seconds?
questions.explanation: The Soviet Union defeated the USA 51-50 after the final 3 seconds were replayed twice, in one of the most controversial endings in Olympic history.

questions.question: Which NBA player is known for scoring 81 points in a single game in 2006?
questions.explanation: Kobe Bryant scored 81 points for the Lakers against the Toronto Raptors on January 22, 2006, the second-highest single-game total in NBA history.

questions.question: Which Lithuanian basketball club has won the most EuroLeague titles?
questions.explanation: Zalgiris Kaunas won the EuroLeague in 1999, the only Lithuanian club to win European basketball's top competition.
